Watch as ragdolls interact with your creations through various AI behaviors. There's no winning or losing—just pure creative experimentation with realistic physics. Place objects, adjust their properties, spawn ragdolls with different behaviors, and observe the hilarious results! ENTER: Start the game ESC: Pause/Unpause R: Restart (from game over) Arrow Keys: Navigate build menu and adjust properties SPACE: Place selected object or spawn ragdoll 1-5: Quick select object types (1=Ragdoll, 2=Cannon, 3=Mine, 4=Fan, 5=Wall) D: Delete mode toggle Z: Clear all objects Testing basic object placement and physics interactions. This verifies that the core mechanics of spawning objects work correctly and that Matter.js physics simulation is functioning properly. Automatically place a variety of objects (ragdolls, cannons, mines, fans, walls) at different positions across the canvas. Trigger cannons to fire and mines to explode, ensuring physics forces are applied correctly. All objects should be placed successfully, ragdolls should react to gravity and forces, cannons should launch projectiles, mines should explode with force, and fans should push nearby objects. No crashes or physics errors should occur. Testing ragdoll AI behaviors and interactions. This ensures that different AI types (Attacker, Seeker, Explorer) function as intended and can navigate and interact with the environment. Spawn multiple ragdolls with different AI behaviors across the map. Place obstacles and interactive objects to create a complex environment. Monitor ragdoll movement patterns and interactions with each other and objects. Attackers should move toward and collide with other ragdolls, Seekers should move around the environment, and Explorers should wander naturally. All ragdolls should respond to physics forces and navigate around obstacles. Testing property adjustment system. This verifies that players can modify object properties like cannon force, mine radius, and ragdoll size, and that these changes affect gameplay correctly. Cycle through different objects and systematically adjust their properties using arrow keys. Spawn objects with modified properties and observe their behavior differences compared to default settings. Property adjustments should be reflected in object behavior—stronger cannons launch farther, larger mines have bigger explosions, and scaled ragdolls have appropriate mass and size changes. UI should accurately display current property values. Testing deletion and clearing functionality. This ensures the delete mode works correctly and that clearing all objects properly resets the game state without causing memory leaks or errors. Place multiple objects, toggle delete mode, remove specific objects by placing the cursor over them and pressing SPACE. Then use the clear all function to remove remaining objects. Repeat the process to verify clean state management. Delete mode should remove individual objects from both the game state and physics world. Clear all should remove every spawned object cleanly. After clearing, new objects should be placeable without errors or performance degradation. Testing collision detection and interaction chains. This verifies that complex multi-object interactions work correctly, including chain reactions from explosions and force propagation. Create a dense cluster of objects including ragdolls, mines, and cannons. Trigger a mine explosion in the center and observe the chain reaction. Test cannon shots hitting multiple ragdolls and other objects. Explosions should affect all objects within radius, causing chain reactions with other mines. Cannon projectiles should transfer momentum to hit objects. Ragdolls should react realistically to all forces, and no collision should be missed or cause errors. Testing edge cases and boundary conditions. This ensures the game handles extreme scenarios gracefully, such as spawning many objects, placing objects at canvas edges, and rapid input sequences. Rapidly spawn maximum objects to test performance limits. Place objects at canvas boundaries and corners. Test quick menu navigation and rapid property adjustments. Attempt to trigger multiple simultaneous explosions. The game should maintain stable performance even with many objects. Boundary-placed objects should stay within canvas bounds or handle edges gracefully. Rapid inputs should be processed correctly without UI glitches or state corruption. Testing game phase transitions and state persistence. This verifies that pausing, unpausing, and restarting work correctly while maintaining proper game state and physics simulation. Build a complex scene, then pause the game and verify physics stops. Unpause and confirm physics resumes correctly. Restart the game and verify all objects are cleared and the start screen displays properly. Pausing should freeze all physics and gameplay while displaying the pause overlay. Unpausing should resume exactly where it left off. Restarting should clear all objects, reset score/state, and return to the start screen cleanly. // globals.js - Global constants and game state export const CANVAS_WIDTH = 600; export const CANVAS_HEIGHT = 400; p.translate(body.position.x, body.position.y); p.rotate(body.angle); if (body.circleRadius) { p.fill(color); p.noStroke(); p.circle(0, 0, body.circleRadius * 2); } else { p.fill(color); p.noStroke(); case AI_BEHAVIORS.SEEKER: return [50, 255, 50]; case AI_BEHAVIORS.EXPLORER: return [50, 150, 255]; default: return [200, 200, 200]; } } update() { if (!this.alive) return; // Simple AI behavior this.moveTimer++; }); } } export class Mine { constructor(p, x, y, radius = 1.0) { this.p = p; this.type = 'mine'; this.explosionRadius = 80 * radius; this.armed = false; this.armTimer = 60; // Arm after 1 second this.exploded = false; Fun with Ragdolls

Create and experiment with physics-based chaos! Build elaborate contraptions using ragdolls, cannons, mines, fans, and other interactive objects. Watch as ragdolls interact with your creations through various AI behaviors. There's no winning or losing—just pure creative experimentation with realistic physics. Place objects, adjust their properties, spawn ragdolls with different behaviors, and observe the hilarious results!

ENTER: Start the game | ESC: Pause/Unpause | R: Restart | Arrow Keys: Navigate build menu and adjust properties | SPACE: Place selected object or spawn ragdoll | 1-5: Quick select object types (1=Ragdoll, 2=Cannon, 3=Mine, 4=Fan, 5=Wall) | D: Delete mode toggle | Z: Clear all objects

test_4_ModeBtn.expected: Delete mode should remove individual objects from both the game state and physics world. Clear all should remove every spawned object cleanly. After clearing, new objects should be placeable without errors or performance degradation.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing collision detection and interaction chains. This verifies that complex multi-object interactions work correctly, including chain reactions from explosions and force propagation.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Create a dense cluster of objects including ragdolls, mines, and cannons. Trigger a mine explosion in the center and observe the chain reaction. Test cannon shots hitting multiple ragdolls and other objects.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Explosions should affect all objects within radius, causing chain reactions with other mines. Cannon projectiles should transfer momentum to hit objects. Ragdolls should react realistically to all forces, and no collision should be missed or cause errors.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing edge cases and boundary conditions. This ensures the game handles extreme scenarios gracefully, such as spawning many objects, placing objects at canvas edges, and rapid input sequences.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: The game should maintain stable performance even with many objects. Boundary-placed objects should stay within canvas bounds or handle edges gracefully. Rapid inputs should be processed correctly without UI glitches or state corruption.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing game phase transitions and state persistence. This verifies that pausing, unpausing, and restarting work correctly while maintaining proper game state and physics simulation.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Build a complex scene, then pause the game and verify physics stops. Unpause and confirm physics resumes correctly. Restart the game and verify all objects are cleared and the start screen displays properly.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Pausing should freeze all physics and gameplay while displaying the pause overlay. Unpausing should resume exactly where it left off. Restarting should clear all objects, reset score/state, and return to the start screen cleanly.
